captures.notes: Canonical Codrops case-study link for attribution and technical framing; local evidence below is generated from scratch for learning review.
Local generated rebuild screenshot showing procedural grass, cosmic beams, interaction wave rings, flower lifecycle markers, and technical world UI labels; not copied from the False Earth demo.
Local generated motion clip isolates grass sway, beam drift, wave pulse, and flower lifecycle changes; not copied from the False Earth demo.
Standalone local HTML/SVG exercise reconstructing the primitive with generated grass blades, cosmic beams, wave rings, flower blooms, and CSS-only drift motion.
Manifest lists only generated local learning evidence plus source-link attribution boundaries.

payload.composition: curved false horizon, ground interaction rings, cosmic beams, dense grass, and lifecycle markers form one technical world interface
payload.visual_role: teaches how a world can behave as an interface rather than a scenic backdrop

modules.review_notes: Complete for first-pass learning: still image, motion clip, and code note all show the same world-state primitive.

payload.bloom_proxy: soft cyan and green halos approximate atmospheric WebGPU material passes
payload.source_boundary: does not claim Codrops compute shaders, raycast code, VAT flower animation, indirect drawing, or renderer setup

modules.review_notes: Shader-material layer is complete enough to teach atmosphere, vegetation, wave, and lifecycle material states separately.

payload.motion_model: CSS drift stands in for wind, beam shimmer, raycast wave pulse, and flower lifecycle updates
payload.runtime_target: offline SVG/CSS review exercise; production analogue would use WebGPU compute, instancing, indirect drawing, and storage buffers

modules.review_notes: Runtime payload is complete because it names bounded counts and the production analogues without claiming upstream implementation.

payload.source_boundary: local generated geometry only; no upstream models, textures, VAT data, or screenshots

modules.review_notes: Asset pipeline is complete because each visible world subsystem has a local generated role.

payload.evidence_layer: local PNG proves world-state composition, local GIF proves motion proxies, HTML note proves the field taxonomy
payload.review_gap: canonical article remains link-only; local rebuild teaches mechanics without upstream media
payload.route_decision: use grass as dense evidence terrain, beams as anomaly markers, waves as reviewer interaction traces, and flower blooms as lifecycle events
payload.source_trail: canonical Codrops case study plus generated local reconstruction
payload.uncertainty: exact upstream WebGPU scale and implementation are not claimed

modules.review_notes: VULCA mapping is complete because source, evidence, review gap, route decision, and uncertainty are all represented.

summary: 2026 WebGPU world case study rebuilt as a local procedural grass, cosmic beam, interaction wave, and flower lifecycle UI-sculpture primitive with still, motion, code, and asset evidence.
title: False Earth
why_relevant: Adds a world-scale technical UI sculpture anchor to the atlas and teaches how WebGPU-era procedural environments can become evidence interfaces.
